rationale: All 23 events (A0, EV-001 through EV-022) show inbound TCP connections to port 3389 (RDP) on host-01.corp.local originating from a single external IP 23.93.242.200, using rapidly incrementing source ports across a ~5.5-minute window — a pattern strongly consistent with an RDP brute-force or credential-stuffing attack. The external IP (23.93.242.200) is not a corporate RFC-1918 address, making this unsolicited inbound RDP access from the internet a high-concern indicator. The burst of connections in the final minute (EV-011 through EV-022, ~14 connections in under 45 seconds) suggests automated tooling. No authentication success/failure events (Windows Security Event 4624/4625) or post-connection process activity are present in this package, so a successful compromise cannot be confirmed, but the volume and pattern of inbound RDP attempts from an external host warrants urgent investigation.
